scenarios.title: 🎒 Dora the Explorer's Backpack Adventure
scenarios.context: Dora is going on a jungle adventure with Boots! Every time she finds a useful item (like a Map or a Golden Key), she packs it into her friendly singing Backpack. The Backpack is represented as a Python List.
scenarios.prompt: How would you use Python List operations (append, remove, indexing, len) to manage items in Dora's Backpack?
objectives: Access the first item using index [0]
scenarios.sampleReasoning: A Python List preserves order. I can create backpack = [], add items with .append(), remove items with .remove(), and inspect contents with indexing and len().

scenarios.title: Ali Baba & The 40 Thieves: Treasure Register
scenarios.context: Ali Baba finds a hidden cave filled with treasures left behind by the 40 thieves. He uses a Python List to store the order of treasures discovered inside.
scenarios.prompt: How would you use a Python List to store the order of treasures found inside the secret cave?
objectives: Store multiple items in an ordered sequence
scenarios.sampleReasoning: I will store all treasure item names inside a single Python List, using list.append() to add each new treasure.

scenarios.title: Ali Baba & The Secret Passwords
scenarios.context: The magic cave door only opens when you speak the exact matching magical command. Ali Baba maps location keys to secret password values.
scenarios.prompt: How would a Python Dictionary help Ali Baba map location names (keys) to secret passwords (values)?
scenarios.sampleReasoning: A Python Dictionary lets me pair key names like "Cave Door" directly with values like "Open Sesame".

scenarios.title: Panchatantra: The Thirsty Crow & Pebble Collection
scenarios.context: A thirsty crow drops pebbles one by one into a water pitcher until the water level rises to the top.
scenarios.prompt: How would you represent pebbles collected in a Python List and count them?
scenarios.sampleReasoning: Start with pebbles = [] and append each pebble collected, checking len(pebbles).

scenarios.title: Panchatantra: The Tortoise & Hare Race Checkpoints
scenarios.context: The judge marks down each race checkpoint the steady tortoise reaches in sequential order from Start to Finish line.
scenarios.prompt: How can a Python List store race checkpoints in order and find out which checkpoint the tortoise is at?
scenarios.sampleReasoning: A Python List preserves the exact order. checkpoints[0] is Start Line and checkpoints[-1] is Finish Line.

scenarios.title: Tenali Rama: Royal Palace Inventory Map
scenarios.context: Tenali Rama audits the royal vault, mapping each room name key to the count of gold vessels stored inside.
scenarios.prompt: Why is a Python Dictionary better than a list when searching for item counts by room name?
scenarios.sampleReasoning: A Python Dictionary allows instant lookup vault["Treasury Room"] to get exact counts.
